source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two small spheres of mass $M$ are suspended between two rigid supports with three identical springs of spring constant $K$ (each of unstretched length $\frac{a}{2}$). Assuming small oscillations about the equilibrium configuration, find the frequencies for the two normal modes corresponding to vertical oscillations.

verifiability: The answer is a compact set of two symbolic frequencies, making final-output checking straightforward.

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Two small spheres of mass $M$ are suspended between two rigid supports with three identical springs of spring constant $K$ (each of unstretched length $\frac{a}{2}$). Assuming small oscillations about the equilibrium configuration, find the frequencies for the two normal modes corresponding to vertical oscillations.

metadata.reasoning_chain: For the vertical oscillations, after defining shifted coordinates $$y_{1}'=y_{1}+\frac{2Mg}{K}, \quad y_{2}'=y_{2}+\frac{2Mg}{K},$$ the two Lagrange equations become $$M\ddot{y}_{1}'+K y_{1}'-\frac{K}{2}y_{2}'=0,\quad M\ddot{y}_{2}'+K y_{2}'-\frac{K}{2}y_{1}'=0.$$ Trying a solution of the type $y_{i}'=B_i e^{i\omega t}$ gives the secular equation $$\begin{vmatrix}K-M\omega^{2} & -\frac{K}{2}\\ -\frac{K}{2} & K-M\omega^{2}\end{vmatrix}=\left(\frac{3}{2}K-M\omega^{2}\right)\left(\frac{1}{2}K-M\omega^{2}\right)=0,$$ which leads to the angular frequencies $$\omega_{3}=\sqrt{\frac{K}{2M}}, \quad \omega_{4}=\sqrt{\frac{3K}{2M}}.$$

problem_main_record.reasoning_chain: For the vertical oscillations, after defining shifted coordinates $$y_{1}'=y_{1}+\frac{2Mg}{K}, \quad y_{2}'=y_{2}+\frac{2Mg}{K},$$ the two Lagrange equations become $$M\ddot{y}_{1}'+K y_{1}'-\frac{K}{2}y_{2}'=0,\quad M\ddot{y}_{2}'+K y_{2}'-\frac{K}{2}y_{1}'=0.$$ Trying a solution of the type $y_{i}'=B_i e^{i\omega t}$ gives the secular equation $$\begin{vmatrix}K-M\omega^{2} & -\frac{K}{2}\\ -\frac{K}{2} & K-M\omega^{2}\end{vmatrix}=\left(\frac{3}{2}K-M\omega^{2}\right)\left(\frac{1}{2}K-M\omega^{2}\right)=0,$$ which leads to the angular frequencies $$\omega_{3}=\sqrt{\frac{K}{2M}}, \quad \omega_{4}=\sqrt{\frac{3K}{2M}}.$$
